item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could lick comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The joke is that its the classic gamer stereotype. Would maybe work better as a -j.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Im in the article and i don't like it
edit: Neutral vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh, ok?
This scip just felt really unfocused and pointless, it reminds me a lot of SCP-1933 but with no real 'story' involved or anything. putting together a bunch of random effects without connection doesn't make something interesting. Also the name feels random for the sake of being random, borderline lolfoundation. Why 'G A M E R M I L K'? Wouldn't something like 'Human Soda Fountain' be more fitting? Nothing here would warrant the name 'Gamermilk'.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is 15 days late (I literally just remembered that I put this here) but I apologize if I sounded harsh and I am aware that it is was 69 net upvotes, I was just making a joke because 69, anyone can upvote or downvote however they please

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well… 69 net upvotes. Since there's downvotes on this page as well, that means that there are more than 69 upvotes in total; it's the combination of up and downvotes that give the current rating.

General note though, voting in a certain way to keep the rating at a specific number is against the rules since it's voting on something other than the content of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This page now has 69 upvotes no one is allowed to upvote or downvote this post

item.title: *Spray Bottle* No
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I have never seen something where every single word, no matter how small, is a hop, skip, and a jump deeper into the mouth of Hell.

And I had to read this on my birthday no less.

+1, reluctant as humanly possible, but still a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If toenails made Baja Blast, it's gotta be some type of Mountain Dew. Gamer Fuel, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Slaps armpit*
Come get ya'll juice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never been more conflicted about whether or not an article deserves an upvote than with this one. Everything about it makes me feel like it deserves a downvote, but for some reason, I clicked the + button. Somehow it works. I will never be able to explain why, but it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could downvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FOOD ITEM: Toenails

SCP-4726 was not provided with toenails during containment.

Shut up and take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course it is, because I know Nico can do better articles than that. It's just the impression than some very good authors are currently favouring low qualities articles rather than the good stuff they usually lay for us. That's a little sad and pretty disappointing

I understand peoples who want to make some fun articles sometimes but, well, there is the -J list for that. That's its job.

Nevermind, my apologises to Nico if I hurt him (I would be glad to give him the fucking upvote mentioned above in case of rewriting. Upvoting is nice), I've spoken to him once on discord and I know he his a good guy (except for his sandbox… Oh Almighty God, please protect us from Nico's sandbox ! This sandbox is a massive deyestruction weapon)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: whyyyyyyy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the primary reason I enjoyed this one was because it didn't do what I expected it to do.

I pull this up and immediately see an image of a big dude vaping in a Hawaiian shirt wearing a fedora, and I was all geared up for some ridiculous fun. And then, it… didn't do that, to say the least. Instead, it got progressively more and more gross as I read.

God imagine lapping up body temperature Mountain Dew off of this guy's body. shudder

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soylent, obviously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have a bad –J in the main series, and you are happy.

1. Authors are allowed to be happy with their work, regardless of what you think.

2. Not everything you disagree with should be a -J.

3. You have a serious case of "attacking the author, not the piece" going on here. Which you should know is against the wiki's rules, since you are a moderator on the French wiki.

I'm not any sort of staff, so you can ignore me if you want, but there's no need to be a dick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since I’ve seen this article get swamped with a ton of responses. I’d figure I throw my hat into the ring and give my two cents worth.

This article is a +1 for me. wow I can feel the glares already The reason for this is I’m found of how the article uses footnotes to advance itself. To be fair, I did find this to be rather short. Though that isn’t an instant down vote for me. Even with how short this article is, it was still entertaining to me.

I think the concept of this article is rather unique. I haven’t seen another scip like this (though I haven’t memorized all the scips on the wiki so I’m not sure if there is or not).

-J talk. I have no clue why people are saying this should be a -J. It’s not funny to me in the slightest, perhaps that why I’m not able to understand it. I find it more disgusting, which I think is a nice feeling for this article.

TL;DR: It’s short and to the point. I got entertainment out of it so I clicked the little plus sign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
insert some vague private joke within

I am really confused where you and others seem to believe there is some sort of in-joke going on here.

Also, I really feel like you could work on the kindness of your language here.

No fucking upvote here

This just seems needlessly harsh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaand that's a downvote.

I'm afraid this article is in line with the current wave of writing a very short -J article with a pretty poor humour and add it to the mainlist.

Ok, that's nice to write an article for oneself and insert some vague private joke within, but I think that's better if the author can write an article, spend time on it and be proud of its work because he knows the readers gonna read it and say : "Wow, this story is fucking good. I'm glad to read stories from a site where authors take so much care of their works. Take my fucking upvote you bastard, you deserve it !"

No fucking upvote here because there is no story and no work. Maybe it could be a nice -J (sorry Nico, 'cause I know you can do better).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me, this article was always meant to be purely a "Gross Out" centered SCP. I can't speak for Nico but I thought the intention was just to make something revolting, which is totally respectable to me.

I don't personally think there's any grand joke here, an inside one or otherwise. The whole thing just makes me gag which, if that was the intention, it succeeded quite spectacularly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for me, but i feel that this belongs in the joke skip section, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have never been so confused by an article on the wiki. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Human flesh. Like squeezing it out of millions of tiny, tiny metal holes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for expressing my opinion in better words than I could. It just feels like a few loose elements that aren’t really linked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because not every article has to be a massive twenty offset 300000 word novella, in fact, a lot of short articles are better than longer ones due to the amount of detail/narrative they can cram into a concise article.

I totally agree with that, but I think the article's shortness doesn't help it.

This is definitely not a -J, not even a bad one.

Well, that's my point. Funny articles can be excellent, but this one justs sounds like somebody telling a friend a private joke you don't know in front of you. I downvoted the ice cream man, and I downvoted this one too. It's still my personal opinion and my goal is not to sound disrespectful to Nico, but I'm quite disappointed in this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I wouldn't say it needs more; I wasn't talking about expanding it, but changing it. Establish a relationship between the input and output, and trim out the energy drink stuff (maybe use the baja blast/toenail thing as your final "wtf?!" stinger).

Honestly, I think it's probably close to the appropriate length for this sort of thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've noticed that short skips with a single concept, while perfectly acceptable, experience a particular phenomenon: if the concept is interesting enough for the reader, great!; if not, there tends to be an expectation that there's either something missing (either from the skip, or from the person's understanding), or that it "needs more" when the problem isn't actually the amount of content but rather the person's taste for the subject matter. (There's a subtle but important difference between 'incomplete' and 'could be expanded on'.)

If a person thinks it needs more, but sees its positive rating, then it's not too far a stretch to assume that there is more, but you're simply not getting it and that the people who do like it are getting it. Also considering how common it is for authors to base skips off of inside jokes among either site people or real life friends, and I can see where many of the comments (beyond the extremes) are coming from. However, this is a self-contained, wholly complete piece. It just doesn't interest me at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Arbys Steaksauce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm mildly amused/irritated by the more negative responses to this article (not all of them; just the ones that seem to go out of their way to be antagonistic). That being said, this didn't work for me at all.

I think if this was more "wtf", it could have; one of the reasons it didn't work for me is because of what he excreted. If there was something more like SCP-914 going on here — with some sort of relationship between what he consumed and what he excreted (implying his body was anomalously "processing" things), I feel like it could have fired off some solid gags (without being too derivative).

Regardless, it's a very strange and silly article that doesn't quite work for me, but doesn't strike me as particularly odious — just half-formed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need a testing log just to see what weird things that come out of him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hotdog water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never before have I come across a comment that so effortlessly manages to display its writers opinions as if they are objective fact rather than just that — an opinion. Bonus points for managing to pull a "SCP is dead" in there. Short, silly articles on the mainlist have been a time honored tradition of this site — we have a Series 1 article that's literally a dildo that vibrates really fast.

Acting like silly fun articles are heralding a decrease in quality for SCPs as a whole is baseless sensationalism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: pastarasta for head admin 2019

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: images of the SCPs themselves, but that would be the only part of the article, just the image only

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about the backstory of this man ? Maybe he was a kid when the anomaly appeared, maybe he had suffering physically or mentaly from this all his life and always today ? Maybe he tried to stop it, maybe he hope to be normal one day with the Foundation, which he believe is an hospital for him ?

These are all pretty uninteresting backstories that would subtract from the tone of the article tbh.

Being convert into salsa with a hammer ? Oh, wait… nevermind.

did someone just diss salsa hammer Side note: if you think salsa hammer just "makes things salsa" then you don't understand a good part of that article.

Why should we develop some kind of deep subject, cross-linking, universe or gods and mythology, if we just have to write a funny thing without reflexion and effort ?

Because not every article has to be a massive twenty offset 300000 word novella, in fact, a lot of short articles are better than longer ones due to the amount of detail/narrative they can cram into a concise article.

You have a bad –J in the main series,

This is definitely not a -J, not even a bad one.

You're obviously entitled to your opinion, but I just can't agree with the attitude that short-funny scips can't be done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Images of what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonder what comes out of him if he eats human flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am affraid of what will look like the next series of SCP.

all SCPs will be replaced will still images that i drew only

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazing.
There is absolutely nothing. The anomalous effect is absurd… I just can't say anything more, this is just ridiculous. He sweats energy drink, and it is the only thing he does.
What about the backstory of this man ? Maybe he was a kid when the anomaly appeared, maybe he had suffering physically or mentaly from this all his life and always today ? Maybe he tried to stop it, maybe he hope to be normal one day with the Foundation, which he believe is an hospital for him ?
But we don't care, uh ? The only things which could makes this thing a little bit interesting are not developped. Here, we're visibly only focus on making something funny. Just take a look on the pic. A fat guy with a katana, who is smoking. Do you really think we will put this picture inside a classified article ? Instead of putting this dude in a standard and clean ouftit in an neutral position to take a good photo ? What the heck.
Hey, what is the next step ? Being convert into salsa with a hammer ? Oh, wait… nevermind.
We already have this kind of a SCP. Yeah, that is amazing. Why should we develop some kind of deep subject, cross-linking, universe or gods and mythology, if we just have to write a funny thing without reflexion and effort ?
The best in this joke, is that everybody seems to like it. You have a bad –J in the main series, and you are happy.
I am affraid of what will look like the next series of SCP.

Of course, I downvoted this thing. I can say whithout any doubt it was one of the worst article I read on the wiki. The only thing I can hope know is to see it being removed.
But I know this will never happen. Hmpf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not super fond of Mr. Gamer Milk, but the toenail bit saved this for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like BERZERKO was talking more about "overweight men with superpowers," which has quite a few entries:

  • SCP-999-J
  • SCP-507
  • SCP-3467-J
  • SCP-893
  • SCP-1788
  • SCP-2611 (not a man, but still)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the article's point is to get a shock reaction, then it does a horrid job of that too. Maybe it's just because I've seen too much shit on here, but I didn't even blink the first time I read it. The second time I read it, which was mostly a double-take response to "why is this so popular," I could see what it was going for, but it fell short of the mark.

I think the reason why that is is because of how underdeveloped the article feels. We're barely introduced to SCP-4726 as a person at all. We don't see how he was found, we don't see the context in which he exists, we don't see his personality… it just cuts straight to the gag. It's like the article is trying to throw a sucker punch, but instead it misses, slips, falls over, and rolls off of a cliff.

I'll leave SCP-1733 here, which I consider to be the gold standard for these kind of "haha wtf" kind of articles. It leads you in with an interesting hook, slowly escalates in a meaningful way, and leaves unsettling implications for the subject. This SCP does none of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the idea here doesn't really engage me, so I can't give this a +1. I think this is because the energy-drink-producing effect is fairly straightforward (at least as straightforward as a scip can be). You feed this guy X and you always get Y. I may not know why that's the case but there's still predictability. Compare that to a scip like 914; you're never sure what is going to come out on the other end, even after two identical inputs. Adding variability to 4726's effect would help, especially if that variability is a reflection of some of 4726's character traits. That said, presentation and overall tone are good, so I can't give this a -1 either.

I can see potential here, so I'm withholding my vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I missing something here, a great reference or even Internet/meme culture? To me it looks just like a rough idea that's not been expended nor developed, and I know you can do way better than that nico.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"overweight-men-who-excrete-consumable-liquids" tag when?

Also, in response to the critique comment:

I kinda like the secreting soda idea but it doesn't go anywhere interesting with it.

I'd say the man eating his own toenails to make mountain dew is pretty interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be a -J.
-1

This isn't criticism. See my comment above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is entertaining but I'd rather it be a J if it exists at all.

I don't really jive with this statement. Just because it doesn't have the same style as other articles on the mainlist doesn't push it into -J territory. The tone present here is out of line with a lot of -Js and the "funny" thing in this article isn't really a joke. While the circumstances or the specific event may be funny, that funny part is not integral to the success of the article. While the shock from the build up and sudden weirdness of the last part is important, you could switch the last thing out with any other strange liquid and it would still retain the core of the article.

Which makes no sense for a -J. When you remove the joke from a -J, you remove the point of the article.

When you remove the "joke" here, you still have that core.

In fact, to make this work as a -J, you would have to completely rework the article from the ground up because the core concept is meant to be horrifying and disgusting, not funny.

So while you can say "I'd rather it be a -J," I don't think that's a very constructive statement considering the piece of work that's been put up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah yes, one of the greatest tropes this wiki has.

Overweight men who excrete consumable liquids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really understanding what the relationship between the inside joke and article is. I really don't think a lack of understanding of the "joke" here would affect the article at all. I don't need to understand that Baja Blast is supposed to be memey to go "what the fuck he eats toe nails then makes a liquid."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, there isn't a whole lot of 'gamer culture' going on here. At least, it isn't grounded in memes. The relationship between this article and greater meme culture is, at most, referential. The only memey thing going on in the actual article is the baja blast, and you do not need to understand that reference at all to go "What the actual fuck."

And while the concept may seem funny, this is way more in the territory of unsettling. You're not supposed to laugh because "ha ha funny joke" you're supposed to laugh because "ha ha what the fuck." That laughter isn't even necessary to the success of the article and is, in the grand scheme of things, secondary to the shock reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the sentiment of it being flat or uninteresting is totally up to you, the observation that it "is just a silly gag that was crammed into a SCP-format casing with the least effort possible" is pretty unfair.

This is meant to just be weird and give insight into a world which gives no fucks. If that doesn't jam with you, that's fine. But to not get it and assume that there has to be something else going on, then to conclude that the piece had little to no effort put into it? Eh.

The specific style this is going for is hard to get right and actually does take effort to toe that line between saying too much and too little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A neckbeard that excretes Moutain Dew.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's just a neckbeard? That's the joke? I kinda like the secreting soda idea but it doesn't go anywhere interesting with it. Other than that it seems overdone.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(European)

24 miles per hour

wtf

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be a -J.

-1
